reasons: Belerick punishes Claude's repeated-hit pattern from basic attacks and Blazing Duet.
Taunt can disrupt Claude when Claude enters teamfight range.

proof.summary: Claude wants to deal repeated hits in close teamfight range, while Belerick punishes frequent damage triggers and can taunt Claude during his commit window.
worksBestWhen: Belerick positions near Claude's intended Blazing Duet target area.
Belerick has enough durability to survive the entry.
failureCases: Claude waits for Belerick's control to be unavailable.
Claude kites from outside Belerick's range.
Belerick is too far from Claude's entry point.

reasons: Kaja can suppress Claude during Blazing Duet.
Claude becomes much easier to kill if he cannot return to or reposition with Battle Mirror Image.

proof.summary: Claude wants to enter, deal AoE damage, and escape with mobility, while Kaja can suppress and drag him during the commit window.
worksBestWhen: Kaja saves Divine Judgment for Claude's real entry.
Allied burst is ready after suppression.
failureCases: Claude baits Kaja with mirror movement.
Kaja cannot reach Claude through frontline.
Kaja suppresses too early and Claude escapes afterward.

reasons: Franco can suppress Claude during Blazing Duet.
Hook also punishes Claude if he shows before mirror reposition is safe.

proof.summary: Claude depends on short mobility and AoE damage windows, while Franco can lock him with suppression and force a failed engage.
worksBestWhen: Franco saves Bloody Hunt until Claude commits.
failureCases: Claude enters after Franco uses suppression.

reasons: Brody can pressure Claude before Claude reaches item comfort.
Brody's heavier early trades and slow make Claude's farming phase more difficult.

proof.summary: Claude wants to scale into attack-speed item spikes, while Brody can pressure early with burst trades, slows, and Abyss Mark damage before Claude's teamfight timing is ready.
worksBestWhen: Brody punishes Claude before Claude has core items.
failureCases: Claude reaches item timing and enters fights after Brody's burst is used.

reasons: Lesley can pressure Claude in lane with long-range poke and camouflage repositioning.
She is a valid gold-lane counter candidate, but the matchup depends heavily on spacing, timing, and user mechanics.

proof.summary: Claude wants to safely farm and scale, while Lesley can use range and camouflage pressure to punish Claude before he reaches strong item timing.
worksBestWhen: Lesley maintains spacing and does not let Claude freely stack or trade.
Lesley uses camouflage to poke and reset lane position.
failureCases: Lesley mispositions and Claude gets mirror engage angle.
Claude safely farms until item spike.
Lesley loses camouflage value by taking or dealing damage at the wrong time.
